div.p: Peak Activation Memory Comparison for Distributed Training
Memory Bottleneck: In GPipe, activations for all M=8 micro-batches must be stored in memory until the backward pass for each micro-batch is completed.
Memory Efficiency: 1F1B only requires P=4 sets of activations at steady state. As soon as a backward pass finishes, its activations are freed.

g.text: Peak Memory Usage | 10.72 GB | 5.36 GB | **50% Reduction**

div.p: GPipe Principle Requires storing all micro-batch activations until the pipeline bubble finishes. Complexity: O(M)
1F1B Principle Interleaves forward and backward passes to release activations earlier. Complexity: O(P)
Crucial Advantage: 1F1B allows for significantly larger M (to reduce bubble overhead) without increasing activation memory consumption.
